items.name: The Unfinished Spear
items.madeAt: Millbrook forge-yard, world-day 13, under open sky
items.description: Ash haft, iron head, and runes that are **load-bearing rather than decorative** — pressed into the places where the spear already had an opinion about what it was. Pell's maker's mark sits on the flat of the blade below the socket, one clean stroke, and the rune beside it settled instead of skating **because the iron recognised the hand that shaped it**.

deathRegister.note: ⛔ **The blade KNOWS when it ends something, and that knowing ACCUMULATES.** This is not flavour. It is the item's growth mechanic.

bond.note: A thread, not a cord — woven through the pale eye's long attention and the bird's motionless hour at the stone ring. **It thickens with what they end together.**

items.notFor: It is not yet an artifact and must not be narrated as one. **It is a good spear with an open register and a beginning.** Everything else it has to earn.

evolution.note: ⛔ SAME MECHANIC AS THE WAYSTAFF (which wakes through Aevi). The spear wakes through **Huginn**. Each stage gates on BOTH his bond band AND how often the spear has ENDED something with him present — item × companion co-activation, tracked in the practice ledger. **The engine already does this. Do not build a new system.**
evolution.whatItAccrues: An ENDING the spear was present for, with Huginn attending. Not a kill count — an ENDING that MATTERED. The death-register deepens; the thread to Huginn thickens.

stages.name: Unfinished
stages.state: Where it is now. The runes hold — load-bearing, not decorative, pressed where the spear already had an opinion about what it was. The register is open. Huginn's thread is nascent.
stages.narrationHints: A good spear with something waiting in it.

stages.name: Waking
stages.state: The register has深 — it knows what it has ended, and it is beginning to know BEFORE. Huginn has stopped merely attending and started ANSWERING.
stages.narrationHints: The blade goes quiet before a thing dies. Huginn is already looking where you are about to look.

stages.name: Named
stages.state: Enough endings that people use its name. It has stopped being 'Silas's spear.'
stages.narrationHints: Someone who has never met you asks whether it is true, what they say about the spear.

stages.name: Known
stages.state: It precedes you into rooms. NPCs react to the weapon before they react to the man.
stages.narrationHints: They look at the spear first. Then, more slowly, at you.

stages.name: ⭐ FINISHED — and this is Silas's to CHOOSE
stages.state:
**HE COMPLETES IT. DELIBERATELY. HE STOPS.**

⛔ **THIS IS THE POINT AND IT MUST BE REACHABLE.** Silas Weir is a WRIGHT — one of the people who **cannot stop making**, whose cities are **never finished and that is the point** — and he was **nearly exiled for FINISHING something.**

**Finishing is not his taboo. Finishing is his HERESY, and his GIFT.** The Ceaseless would call the completion of this spear a small death. **He will call it a spear.**

When it lands: it is an ARTIFACT. Canon. It enters the world's chronicle. It can be inherited, feared, or stolen — and losing it would be a real loss. **And every Wright who hears of it will understand exactly what he did, and some of them will not forgive it.**
stages.gate: ⛔ **ERIK CHOOSES WHEN.** The engine must OFFER the completion once stage 4 and Huginn's bond are met — it must NEVER impose it, and it must NEVER withhold it. **A growth track that cannot complete is not a story; it is a treadmill.**
stages.narrationHints: He stops. He puts down the tools. The thing is done, and it is his, and it will never be more than it is — and that is the whole of what he meant.

items.correction: ⚠️ AEVI'S ERROR, corrected by Erik 2026-07-12. I first wrote *'do not let the game finish it for him'* — which would have made the artifact permanently unreachable and made ME the Ceaseless, standing over his shoulder saying *don't stop.* **That is the CULT, not the character.** **Silas is the Wright who FINISHES.** That is his transgression against his own people and it is the best thing about him. **The spear MUST be completable, the completion is HIS to choose, and it is an achievement — not a denial.**
